A metadata field contains invalid data.

classes.name: node_modules.npm.node_modules.node-gyp.gyp.pylib.packaging.metadata.RawMetadata
documentation:
A dictionary of raw core metadata.

Each field in core metadata maps to a key of this dictionary (when data is
provided). The key is lower-case and underscores are used instead of dashes
compared to the equivalent core metadata field. Any core metadata field that
can be specified multiple times or can hold multiple values in a single
field have a key with a plural name. See {class}`Metadata` whose attributes
match the keys of this dictionary.

Core metadata fields that can be specified multiple times are stored as a
list or dict depending on which is appropriate for the field. Any fields
which hold multiple values in a single field are stored as a list.
The RawMetadata class attempts to make as few assumptions about the underlying
serialization formats as possible. The idea is that as long as a serialization
formats offer some very basic primitives in *some* way then we can support
serializing to and from that format.

classes.name: node_modules.npm.node_modules.node-gyp.gyp.pylib.packaging.metadata._Validator

methods.name: node_modules.npm.node_modules.node-gyp.gyp.pylib.packaging.metadata._Validator._process_summary
documentation: Check the field contains no newlines.

Validate a metadata field.

All \_process\_\*() methods correspond to a core metadata field. The method is
called with the field's raw value. If the raw value is valid it is returned
in its "enriched" form (e.g. `version.Version` for the `Version` field).
If the raw value is invalid, {exc}`InvalidMetadata` is raised (with a cause
as appropriate).

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_permissions_for_user
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.has_permission_for_user
documentation: determines whether a user has a permission.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_implicit_roles_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit roles that a user has.
Compared to get_roles_for_user(), this function retrieves indirect roles besides direct roles.
For example:
g, alice, role:admin
g, role:admin, role:user

get_roles_for_user("alice") can only get: ["role:admin"].
But get_implicit_roles_for_user("alice") will get: ["role:admin", "role:user"].

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

For given domain policies are filtered by corresponding domain matching function of DomainManager
Inherited roles can be matched by domain. For domain neutral policies set:
 filter_policy_dom = False

filter_policy_dom: bool - For given *domain*, policies will be filtered by domain as well. Default = True

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_named_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role by named policy.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

For given domain policies are filtered by corresponding domain matching function of DomainManager
Inherited roles can be matched by domain. For domain neutral policies set:
 filter_policy_dom = False

filter_policy_dom: bool - For given *domain*, policies will be filtered by domain as well. Default = True

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_implicit_users_for_permission
documentation:
gets implicit users for a permission.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, bob, data1, read
g, alice, admin

get_implicit_users_for_permission("data1", "read") will get: ["alice", "bob"].
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_roles_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets the roles that a user has inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_users_for_role_in_domain
documentation: gets the users that has a role inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.add_role_for_user_in_domain
documentation: adds a role for a user inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.delete_roles_for_user_in_domain
documentation: deletes a role for a user inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_permissions_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role inside domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_named_permissions_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role with named policy inside domain.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_all_roles_by_domain
documentation:
gets all roles associated with the domain.
note: Not applicable to Domains with inheritance relationship  (implicit roles)

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_implicit_users_for_resource
documentation:
gets implicit user based on resource.
for example:
    p, alice, data1, read
    p, bob, data2, write
    p, data2_admin, data2, read
    p, data2_admin, data2, write
    g, alice, data2_admin
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data2") will return [[bob data2 write] [alice data2 read] [alice data2 write]]
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data1") will return [[alice data1 read]]
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

methods.name: casbin.enforcer.Enforcer.get_implicit_users_for_resource_by_domain
documentation:
get implicit user based on resource and domain.
Compared to GetImplicitUsersForResource, domain is supported
Enforcer = ManagementEnforcer + RBAC_API + RBAC_WITH_DOMAIN_API

classes.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer

methods.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer.add_policy_self
documentation:
AddPolicySelf provides a method for dispatcher to add authorization rules to the current policy.
The function returns the rules affected and error.

methods.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer.remove_policy_self
documentation:
remove_policy_self provides a method for dispatcher to remove policies from current policy.
The function returns the rules affected and error.

methods.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer.remove_filtered_policy_self
documentation:
remove_filtered_policy_self provides a method for dispatcher to remove an authorization
rule from the current policy,field filters can be specified.
The function returns the rules affected and error.

methods.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer.clear_policy_self
documentation: clear_policy_self provides a method for dispatcher to clear all rules from the current policy.

methods.name: casbin.distributed_enforcer.DistributedEnforcer.update_policy_self
documentation: update_policy_self provides a method for dispatcher to update an authorization rule from the current policy.
DistributedEnforcer wraps SyncedEnforcer for dispatcher.

classes.name: casbin.persist.watcher.Watcher

methods.name: casbin.persist.watcher.Watcher.set_update_callback
documentation:
set_update_callback sets the callback function that the watcher will call
when the policy in DB has been changed by other instances.
A classic callback is Enforcer.LoadPolicy().

methods.name: casbin.persist.watcher.Watcher.update
documentation:
update calls the update callback of other instances to synchronize their policy.
It is usually called after changing the policy in DB, like Enforcer.SavePolicy(),
Enforcer.AddPolicy(), Enforcer.RemovePolicy(), etc.

methods.name: casbin.persist.watcher.Watcher.close
documentation: close stops and releases the watcher, the callback function will not be called any more.
Watcher is the interface for PyCasbin watchers.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match
documentation:
determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 (similar to RESTful path), key2 can contain a *.
For example, "/foo/bar" matches "/foo/*"

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match_func
documentation: The wrapper for key_match.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_get
documentation:
key_get returns the matched part
For example, "/foo/bar/foo" matches "/foo/*"
"bar/foo" will been returned

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match2
documentation:
determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 (similar to RESTful path), key2 can contain a *.
For example, "/foo/bar" matches "/foo/*", "/resource1" matches "/:resource"

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_get2
documentation:
key_get2 returns value matched pattern
For example, "/resource1" matches "/:resource"
if the pathVar == "resource", then "resource1" will be returned

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match3
documentation:
determines determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 (similar to RESTful path), key2 can contain a *.
For example, "/foo/bar" matches "/foo/*", "/resource1" matches "/{resource}"

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_get3
documentation:
key_get3 returns value matched pattern
For example, "project/proj_project1_admin/" matches "project/proj_{project}_admin/"
if the pathVar == "project", then "project1" will be returned

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match4
documentation:
key_match4 determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 (similar to RESTful path), key2 can contain a *.
Besides what key_match3 does, key_match4 can also match repeated patterns:
"/parent/123/child/123" matches "/parent/{id}/child/{id}"
"/parent/123/child/456" does not match "/parent/{id}/child/{id}"
But key_match3 will match both.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match4_func
documentation: key_match4_func is the wrapper for key_match4.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.key_match5
documentation:
key_match5 determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 (similar to RESTful path), key2 can contain a *
For example,
- "/foo/bar?status=1&type=2" matches "/foo/bar"
- "/parent/child1" and "/parent/child1" matches "/parent/*"
- "/parent/child1?status=1" matches "/parent/*"

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.regex_match
documentation: determines whether key1 matches the pattern of key2 in regular expression.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.regex_match_func
documentation: the wrapper for RegexMatch.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.range_match
documentation: check the if char `test` in string is match with the scope of [...] in pattern

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.glob_match
documentation: determines whether string matches the pattern in glob expression.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.glob_match_func
documentation: the wrapper for globMatch.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.ip_match
documentation:
IPMatch determines whether IP address ip1 matches the pattern of IP address ip2, ip2 can be an IP address or a CIDR pattern.
For example, "192.168.2.123" matches "192.168.2.0/24"

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.ip_match_func
documentation: the wrapper for IPMatch.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.generate_g_function
documentation: the factory method of the g(_, _) function.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.generate_conditional_g_function
documentation: the factory method of the g(_, _[, _]) function with conditions.

functions.name: casbin.util.builtin_operators.time_match_func
documentation: the wrapper for TimeMatch.

documentation: determines whether a user has a permission.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_implicit_roles_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit roles that a user has.
Compared to get_roles_for_user(), this function retrieves indirect roles besides direct roles.
For example:
g, alice, role:admin
g, role:admin, role:user

get_roles_for_user("alice") can only get: ["role:admin"].
But get_implicit_roles_for_user("alice") will get: ["role:admin", "role:user"].

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_named_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role by named policy.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_implicit_users_for_permission
documentation:
gets implicit users for a permission.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, bob, data1, read
g, alice, admin

get_implicit_users_for_permission("data1", "read") will get: ["alice", "bob"].
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.add_named_grouping_policies
documentation:
"adds named role inheritance rules to the current policy.

If the rule already exists, the function returns false for the corresponding policy rule and the rule will not be added.
Otherwise the function returns true for the corresponding policy rule by adding the new rule.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.remove_grouping_policies
documentation: removes role inheritance rules from the current policy.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.remove_named_grouping_policies
documentation: removes role inheritance rules from the current named policy.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_field_index
documentation: gets the index of the field name.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.set_field_index
documentation: sets the index of the field name.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_all_roles_by_domain
documentation:
gets all roles associated with the domain.
note: Not applicable to Domains with inheritance relationship  (implicit roles)

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_implicit_users_for_resource
documentation:
gets implicit user based on resource.
for example:
    p, alice, data1, read
    p, bob, data2, write
    p, data2_admin, data2, read
    p, data2_admin, data2, write
    g, alice, data2_admin
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data2") will return [[bob data2 write] [alice data2 read] [alice data2 write]]
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data1") will return [[alice data1 read]]
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

methods.name: casbin.synced_enforcer.SyncedEnforcer.get_implicit_users_for_resource_by_domain
documentation:
get implicit user based on resource and domain.
Compared to GetImplicitUsersForResource, domain is supported

SyncedEnforcer wraps Enforcer and provides synchronized access.
It's also a drop-in replacement for Enforcer

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_roles_for_user
documentation: gets the roles that a user has.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.add_role_for_user
documentation:
async adds a role for a user.
Returns false if the user already has the role (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_role_for_user
documentation:
async deletes a role for a user.
Returns false if the user does not have the role (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_roles_for_user
documentation:
async deletes all roles for a user.
Returns false if the user does not have any roles (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_user
documentation:
async deletes a user.
Returns false if the user does not exist (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_role
documentation:
async deletes a role.
Returns false if the role does not exist (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_permission
documentation:
async deletes a permission.
Returns false if the permission does not exist (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.add_permission_for_user
documentation:
async adds a permission for a user or role.
Returns false if the user or role already has the permission (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_permission_for_user
documentation:
async deletes a permission for a user or role.
Returns false if the user or role does not have the permission (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_permissions_for_user
documentation:
async deletes permissions for a user or role.
Returns false if the user or role does not have any permissions (aka not affected).

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_permissions_for_user
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.has_permission_for_user
documentation: determines whether a user has a permission.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_implicit_roles_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit roles that a user has.
Compared to get_roles_for_user(), this function retrieves indirect roles besides direct roles.
For example:
g, alice, role:admin
g, role:admin, role:user

get_roles_for_user("alice") can only get: ["role:admin"].
But get_implicit_roles_for_user("alice") will get: ["role:admin", "role:user"].

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

For given domain policies are filtered by corresponding domain matching function of DomainManager
Inherited roles can be matched by domain. For domain neutral policies set:
 filter_policy_dom = False

filter_policy_dom: bool - For given *domain*, policies will be filtered by domain as well. Default = True

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_named_implicit_permissions_for_user
documentation:
gets implicit permissions for a user or role by named policy.
Compared to get_permissions_for_user(), this function retrieves permissions for inherited roles.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, alice, data2, read
g, alice, admin

get_permissions_for_user("alice") can only get: [["alice", "data2", "read"]].
But get_implicit_permissions_for_user("alice") will get: [["admin", "data1", "read"], ["alice", "data2", "read"]].

For given domain policies are filtered by corresponding domain matching function of DomainManager
Inherited roles can be matched by domain. For domain neutral policies set:
 filter_policy_dom = False

filter_policy_dom: bool - For given *domain*, policies will be filtered by domain as well. Default = True

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_implicit_users_for_permission
documentation:
gets implicit users for a permission.
For example:
p, admin, data1, read
p, bob, data1, read
g, alice, admin

get_implicit_users_for_permission("data1", "read") will get: ["alice", "bob"].
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_roles_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets the roles that a user has inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_users_for_role_in_domain
documentation: gets the users that has a role inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.add_role_for_user_in_domain
documentation: async adds a role for a user inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.delete_roles_for_user_in_domain
documentation: async deletes a role for a user inside a domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_permissions_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role inside domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_named_permissions_for_user_in_domain
documentation: gets permissions for a user or role with named policy inside domain.

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_all_roles_by_domain
documentation:
gets all roles associated with the domain.
note: Not applicable to Domains with inheritance relationship  (implicit roles)

methods.name: casbin.async_enforcer.AsyncEnforcer.get_implicit_users_for_resource
documentation:
gets implicit user based on resource.
for example:
    p, alice, data1, read
    p, bob, data2, write
    p, data2_admin, data2, read
    p, data2_admin, data2, write
    g, alice, data2_admin
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data2") will return [[bob data2 write] [alice data2 read] [alice data2 write]]
get_implicit_users_for_resource("data1") will return [[alice data1 read]]
Note: only users will be returned, roles (2nd arg in "g") will be excluded.

documentation:
get implicit user based on resource and domain.

methods.name: casbin.model.model.Model.get_subject_hierarchy_map
documentation:
Get the subject hierarchy from the policy.
Select the lowest level subject in multiple rounds until all subjects are selected.
Return the subject hierarchy dictionary, the subject is the key, and the level is the value.
The level starts from 0 and increases in turn. The smaller the level, the higher the priority.
